title: Custom Queries — Blog Post API
description:
### Your Task

Build a Blog Post CRUD API with custom repository finder methods.

### Requirements

- `BlogPost` entity: `id` (Long), `title` (String), `content` (String), `author` (String), `category` (String), `published` (Boolean)
- `BlogPostRepository` with custom finders:
  - `findByAuthor(String author)` → List<BlogPost>
  - `findByCategory(String category)` → List<BlogPost>
  - `findByPublishedTrue()` → List<BlogPost>
- `BlogPostController` at `/api/posts`

### Endpoints

- `GET /api/posts` → all posts (200)
- `GET /api/posts/{id}` → by ID (200 or 404)
- `GET /api/posts/author/{author}` → posts by author (200)
- `GET /api/posts/category/{category}` → posts by category (200)
- `GET /api/posts/published` → only published posts (200)
- `POST /api/posts` → create (201)
- `PUT /api/posts/{id}` → update (200 or 404)
- `DELETE /api/posts/{id}` → delete (204 or 404)

### Key Concepts

- Spring Data JPA derived query methods — just name the method correctly!
- `findByAuthor` → Spring auto-generates the SQL WHERE clause
- No @Query annotation needed for simple finders
hints: Spring Data generates queries from method names: findByAuthor → WHERE author = ?
These are just interface methods — no implementation needed
